data.title: Joby Aviation Logs 50,000 Miles In Electric Skies Tour
data.description:
Joby Aviation (NYSE:JOBY) shares are rising in Friday's premarket trading after the company completed a series of demonstration flights across the San Francisco Bay Area as part of its 2026 Electric Skies Tour.

The flights showcased Joby's operational readiness for its electric air taxi service, highlighting its potential to ease congestion in busy urban corridors. The aircraft also flew near the Golden Gate Bridge, marking a milestone in the company's push to make short-distance aerial commuting viable.

Joby has logged more than 50,000 miles across its fleet and is working toward certification while scaling production. The company plans to expand manufacturing through facilities in Dayton, Ohio; Marina, California; and San Carlos, California.

Its capacity is expected to reach up to four aircraft per month by 2027 and as many as 500 annually at the Dayton site.

The company is also building an urban air mobility ecosystem through partnerships with Uber and Delta Air Lines.

Commencement of Testing Air Taxi & FAA Partnership

This week, the company has revealed it has begun testing the production version of its upcoming air taxi amid a partnership with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).

Joby also announced it was partnering with the FAA and participating in the eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP). The program would enable the company to begin operations in 2026 in 10 states across the U.S.

Technical Analysis

The broader market experienced gains on the previous trading day, with the S&P 500 closing up 0.40% and the Technology sector rising 0.44%. Joby’s rise comes as the market showed positive momentum, suggesting that the stock is moving in line with broader trends.

Currently, Joby Aviation is trading 0.5% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and significantly below its 50-day SMA by 17.2%. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 62.60%, and the stock is positioned closer to its 52-week lows than highs, indicating potential challenges ahead.

The RSI is at 42.26, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.4734, with the signal line at -0.6227, indicating a bullish scenario as the MACD is above the signal line.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is not in a strong trend, there may be potential for upward movement.

  • Key Resistance: $11.00
  • Key Support: $9.50

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Joby Aviation, Inc. is slated to provide its next financial update on May 6, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 22 cents (Down from Loss of 11 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $19.70 million (Up from $0.00 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $12.50. Recent analyst moves include:

  • JP Morgan: Underweight (Lowers Target to $7.00) (Feb. 26)
  • Needham: Buy (Lowers Target to $18.00) (Feb. 26)
  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Upgraded to Buy (Target $18.00) (Feb. 26)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Joby Aviation, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Strong (Score: 73.44) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Joby Aviation’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, suggesting that the stock is currently outperforming its peers. However, investors should remain cautious given the mixed technical indicators and the proximity to key support levels.

data.title: ServiceTitan Shares Slide After Q4 Earnings Report, Analysts Cut Price Targets
data.description:
ServiceTitan, Inc. (NASDAQ:TTAN) shares are trading lower on Friday after the company reported fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday after the market closed. Also, Keybanc and Needham lowered their respective price targets on the stock.

  • ServiceTitan stock is taking a hit today. What’s pressuring TTAN stock?

Q4 Results And Key Metrics

ServiceTitan reported adjusted earnings per share of 27 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 18 cents. In addition, it reported revenue of $253.98 million, beating the consensus estimate of $245.47 million.

Platform revenue came in at $245.1 million, compared with $200.1 million in the prior-year quarter, representing 23% year-over-year growth.

Gross transaction volume totaled $19.8 billion in the fourth quarter, up from $17.0 billion a year earlier.

Total active customers increased to approximately 10,800, up from about 9,500 a year earlier. Net dollar retention remained above 110%, while gross dollar retention stayed above 95%.

“Our vision and mission from the beginning have been simple: to inflect our customers’ revenue and margins by automating their operations” said Ara Mahdessian, Co-Founder and CEO. “I am deeply proud to surpass a $1B annualized revenue run rate and to see our vision unfold faster than we ever could have imagined.”

ServiceTitan sees first-quarter revenue of $255.00 million to $257.00 million, versus the consensus estimate of $252.02 million. Furthermore, it sees fiscal-year revenue of $1.11 billion to $1.12 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $1.09 billion.

Consensus Ratings And Analyst Actions

The company has a consensus rating of Buy and a consensus price target of $127.94. Recent analyst changes include:

  • Needham analyst Scott Berg maintained a Buy rating on ServiceTitan and lowered the price target from $140 to $100.
  • Keybanc analyst Jason Celino maintained an Overweight rating on ServiceTitan and lowered the price target from $140 to $120.

ServiceTitan Stock Drops

TTAN Price Action: At the time of publication, ServiceTitan shares are trading 4.85% lower at $71.98, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

data.title: What's Going On With 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals On Friday?
data.description:
60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals Inc. (NASDAQ:SXTP) shares are down during Friday’s premarket session. This follows a 9.6% decline, closing at $2.91 on Thursday.

On Wednesday, the stock surged almost 48% as the company announced that all patients in its babesiosis trial were cured after tafenoquine treatment.

Babesiosis is a malaria-like parasitic infection. It is caused by Babesia parasites that infect red blood cells, primarily transmitted by Ixodes scapularis (blacklegged/deer) ticks.

100% Cure Rate For Tick Disease

The company’s expanded use trial evaluated the ARAKODA regimen of tafenoquine combined with atovaquone and other antimalarials/antibiotics in patients with risk factors for severe disease with relapsing babesiosis who have previously failed conventional antimicrobial regimens.

The company highlighted the cure rate for relapsing babesiosis in immunosuppressed patients approaches 100% when tafenoquine is added to a patient’s treatment regimen.

Tafenoquine is currently approved for malaria prophylaxis under the name ARAKODA, but it is not yet FDA-approved for treating babesiosis.

60 Degrees Partners With Runway Health on ARAKODA Access

In January, 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals partnered with Runway Health to expand pre-departure access to ARAKODA by integrating a travel-focused telehealth platform that lets international travelers obtain physician-led online consultations before visiting malaria-endemic regions.

60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $17.60. Recent analyst moves include:

  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Raises Target to $24.00) (Jan. 27)
  • Ascendiant Capital: Buy (Lowers Target to $11.20) (Nov. 28, 2025)

60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

The Verdict: 60 Degrees Pharmaceuticals’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the weak momentum score indicates challenges, the recent trial results may provide a catalyst for future growth.

data.title: Over 50% of Americans Made 'Risky Financial Stopgaps' Last Year — 20% Even Skipped Medical Care
data.description:
Quick Summary

  • More than half of Americans used at least one risky financial stopgap late last year, with many cutting essentials, missing debt payments or even skipping medical care to stay afloat.
  • For borrowers buried under high-interest balances, you can compare lenders and personalized loan options in minutes without affecting credit scores.

For a growing share of Americans, the numbers don't line up anymore.

A survey issued by Achieve found that in the last three months of 2025, 51% resorted to at least one "risky financial stopgap" after falling short on what they already owed. Half of those who took action cut spending on basic needs, 34% leaned harder on credit cards and 26% pulled money from emergency or short‑term savings.

"This is what the K-shaped economy looks like in the real world," said Achieve Co-CEO Andrew Housser. "There's an affluent half of the population whose financial lives aren't disrupted by momentary inconveniences. But for everyone else, financial triage and tradeoffs are a way of life."

Belt‑tightening down to the last notch

The survey suggests many households have already exhausted easy ways to cut back. More than 9 in 10 said they could only reduce housing costs, bills and utilities, groceries, or transportation "slightly" or not at all. Even in discretionary areas, most people feel tapped out.

Seventy five percent said they were out of room to cut entertainment at home, 59% said the same about gift‑giving, 56% about entertainment outside the home and 55% about vacations. When the budget side is fully squeezed, the only remaining lever is often the cost of existing debt, not whether you owe it, but what rate you're paying and how it's structured.

"Many consumers' belt‑tightening efforts have reached the last notch," Housser noted, warning that staying current on debts gets harder when there's no slack left and prices or payments rise. That lack of margin also makes households more vulnerable to routine surprises like car repairs or medical bills.​

The survey backs that up. Fifty‑five percent of respondents said they carry credit card balances to cover essential expenses, and more than a quarter have kept those balances for at least six months. 

Overall, 55% rated their financial situation as "Poor" or "Fair," while just 13% said "Excellent."​

Debt trends show slow progress 

Achieve's report does show some mixed signals. Over the prior three months, 24% of respondents saw their total debt increase, while 32% saw it decrease and 44% said it stayed the same. That's a slight improvement from the prior quarter, when 27% reported rising debt and 42% said it was flat.

Put together, the data sets an illustration that some households are slowly digging out, but a large share are stuck cycling balances just to keep up with essentials, with little room for error.

A more deliberate way to reset

There's no single fix for a cost‑of‑living squeeze. But when high‑interest debt is at the center of the problem, the order of operations matters:

Get a clear picture of what you owe and to whom. Prioritize the most expensive balances and the most urgent obligations, and look for ways to lower the cost of that debt—through rate reductions, structured payoff plans—before it forces more skipped bills or medical delays.

This is where some households turn to personal loans or consolidation loans to replace multiple high‑interest balances with a single, fixed payment. 

In a year when more than half of Americans say they've already resorted to risky stopgaps, moving from reactive choices to deliberate ones may be an important shift.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Kratos, Airbus Collaborate On Advanced Uncrewed Combat Aircraft
data.description:
Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ:KTOS) shares are up during Friday's premarket session. The company disclosed that it is preparing two Valkyries for their first flight with a European mission system.

This positive movement comes as Kratos continues its collaboration with Airbus SE (OTC:EADSY). The deal entails developing an integrated Uncrewed Collaborative Combat Aircraft (UCCA) for the German Air Force.

Details

The joint effort aims to deliver a proven combat aircraft that integrates with Airbus's Multiplatform Autonomous Reconfigurable and Secure (MARS) system.

The Valkyrie has a range exceeding 5,000 kilometers and is capable of flying at altitudes up to 45,000 feet. It is designed to undertake sensitive missions that would be too dangerous for pilots.

In addition to the technical specifications, the partnership emphasizes affordability and rapid deployment, addressing urgent defense needs in Europe.

The Valkyrie first flew in the U.S. in 2019, with additional aircraft operating regularly since then. The Airbus variant is set to make its maiden flight in 2026.

As the maiden flight approaches, Kratos is preparing two Valkyries in Manching, Germany.

Management Commentary

Steve Fendley, President of Kratos Unmanned Systems Division, added, "By taking the flight-proven and in-production Valkyrie and integrating the Airbus MARS mission system, the Airbus-missionized Valkyrie UCCA is a multi-mission, affordable system that can operate independently, in teams of UAS, or in Manned-Unmanned-Teaming operations."

"Along with the technical and production backing Airbus and Kratos bring, we are realizing an optimal capability system that can be bought and deployed as ‘affordable mass'; the consistent discriminator identified in today's peer to peer wargames."

Technical Analysis

Currently, the stock is trading 1.6% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 1% above its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term weakness but longer-term strength. Over the past 12 months, shares have surged by 203.05%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting strong overall performance.

The RSI is at 47.93, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at -2.3361, with the signal line at -2.5083, indicating bullish momentum as the MACD is above the signal line.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is not currently overbought, there is potential for upward movement.

  • Key Resistance: $97.00
  • Key Support: $84.50

Analyst Outlook

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $91.47. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Canaccord Genuity: Buy (Raises Target to $125.00) (Feb. 24)
  • BTIG: Buy (Raises Target to $115.00) (Feb. 24)
